tags.name: OrdersService
tags.description: The Orders Service provides APIs for managing orders in Zentail for a sales channel / listing integration.

get.summary:
GetOrder returns a single order by one of its identifiers, in the same
lean ShippedOrder view ListShippedOrders returns. Unlike ListShippedOrders
it applies no status filter, returning the order whatever its status, so a
caller (e.g. the shipment-confirmation CLI) can fetch a specific order by
channel order id, Zentail order number, or Zentail order id. The
storefront / integration is resolved from the API token; a channel_order_id
lookup is scoped to that integration. Returns NOT_FOUND when no order
matches.

parameters.name: channelOrderId
parameters.description:
The order ID used by the channel when the order was created
(reseller_order_id), scoped to the token's integration.

post.summary: CancelItems cancels items in an order.

get.summary:
ListShippedOrders returns orders whose internal Zentail status is
SHIPPED or PARTIALLY_SHIPPED, for shipment-confirmation polling.
get.description:
The internal status filter is enforced server-side; callers cannot
request other statuses. Channel-fulfilled orders (FBA / AFN) are
excluded by default, since the seller does not confirm shipment for
them; set include_channel_fulfilled_orders to include them. Results
are keyset-paginated: pass the next_page_cursor from the previous
response to fetch the next page. An empty next_page_cursor means there
are no more results. The storefront / integration is resolved from the
API token, not the request.

parameters.name: lastUpdatedTs
parameters.description:
Only return orders whose last update is at or after this time
(inclusive). Maps to the channel-order last update timestamp; named
to match the existing salesOrder list wording (lastUpdatedTs).

parameters.name: salesChannelStatus
parameters.description:
Optional additional filter on the sales-channel status (the
reseller_status column), e.g. the channel's own shipped status.
The internal SHIPPED / PARTIALLY_SHIPPED filter is always applied
regardless of this value.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
Opaque pagination cursor. Empty for the first page; otherwise the
next_page_cursor returned by the previous response.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Maximum number of orders to return. Zero or negative uses the
server default; the server may clamp to a maximum.

parameters.name: includeChannelFulfilledOrders
parameters.description:
Include channel-fulfilled (FBA / AFN) orders. Defaults to false: such
orders are excluded because the seller does not confirm shipment for
them. Set true to include them anyway.

channelOrderId.title: The order ID used by your channel when creating the order

cancelQuantities.title: A map of line item IDs to quantities to cancel

orders_apiCancelItemsRequest.description: CancelItemsRequest is the request to cancel items in an order.

orders_apiCancelItemsResponse.description: CancelItemsResponse is the response to a CancelItems request.

orders_apiGetOrderResponse.description: GetOrderResponse carries the requested order in the lean ShippedOrder view.

nextPageCursor.description:
The cursor token for the next page of results.
If empty, there are no more results.

orders_apiListShippedOrdersResponse.description: ListShippedOrdersResponse is a single page of shipped orders.

channelOrderId.description: The order ID used by the channel when the order was created.

status.description: Internal Zentail order status: SHIPPED or PARTIALLY_SHIPPED.

lastUpdatedTs.description: When the order was last updated.

marketplaceId.description: The sales channel's marketplace id (e.g. the Amazon marketplace).

standardServiceLevel.description:
The standardized requested service level, used as a carrier-mapping
fallback when a package carries no service level of its own.

lineItems.description:
Order-level line items, carrying the channel line-item id and ordered
quantity needed to map shipped packages back to channel order items.

packages.description:
Shipped packages (one per tracking number) with their per-line shipped
quantities — the unit of shipment confirmation.

orders_apiShippedOrder.description:
ShippedOrder is a lean view of a shipped / partially-shipped order,
carrying only the fields a shipment-confirmation poller needs.

lineItemId.description:
The Zentail line item id (purchase_order_product id), as a string to match
the channel-facing representation.

channelLineItemId.description: The channel's own line item id, when the channel assigns one.

quantity.description: The ordered quantity for this line.

orders_apiShippedOrderLineItem.description:
ShippedOrderLineItem is an order-level line item (not package-scoped),
carrying the identifiers needed to resolve a shipped package line back to
the channel's order item.

items.description: The line items (and shipped quantities) in this package.

orders_apiShippedOrderPackage.description:
ShippedOrderPackage is a single shipped package (one tracking number) with
the line quantities it shipped.

lineItemId.description:
The Zentail line item id (purchase_order_product id); matches
ShippedOrderLineItem.line_item_id.

quantity.description: The quantity of this line shipped in this package.

orders_apiShippedOrderPackageItem.description: ShippedOrderPackageItem is one line's shipped quantity within a package.
